business_ideas.idea: OpenSea: NFT marketplace for digital collectibles
Remote work lifestyle platforms (nomadlist, remoteok, other digital products)
Microacquire: marketplace to buy/sell profitable SaaS businesses
Private jet photo studio (fake interior for influencer Instagram photos)
American Striping: parking lot and road line painting business
At-home diagnostic testing kits and services
Luna/Terra: stable coin infrastructure for crypto payments

predictions.text: San Francisco will bounce back and remain tech center for decades.
Global salary arbitrage will democratize work; US tech salaries will decline.
Winklevoss twins end up wealthier than Zuckerberg through Bitcoin and Gemini exchange.
At-home diagnostics industry will continue to boom significantly.
VR will continue rapid growth despite mainstream skepticism and discomfort issues.

disagreements: Shaan believes high salary inflation will reverse as companies discover offshore talent; Sam thinks this won't happen in 2022. Shaan bullish on crypto/Luna; others (particularly around NFTs/digital art value bets) express skepticism about crypto sustainability.
dynamics: This is the annual 'Milly Awards' ceremony where hosts rate companies, people, and trends across categories. Rapid-fire discussion of dozens of businesses with quick reactions and scoring. Sam often brings playful skepticism; Shaan goes deep on financials and numbers. High energy, competitive tone as hosts try to pick original winners vs. obvious ones. Lots of tangential stories (Nick Huber mattress, etc.).
frameworks: Bet on yourself — Elon's Tesla compensation tied to stock milestones; true conviction aligns personal incentives.
Seek unsexy, profitable businesses — Line painting, vending machines, negotiation services outperform hype-driven startups.
Who do you employ? — Target underserved workforces (stay-at-home moms, ex-cons, military spouses) for culture + scale.
Global talent arbitrage — Offshore talent (India/Philippines) at 1/5-1/6 cost with higher motivation and skill.
learnings: NFT market is real ($15B OpenSea GMV in 2021 vs $24M in 2020) but technology is half-baked; protocols will change.
One-person profitable internet businesses can scale to $5M revenue (Peter Levels); bootstrapped models rival VC-backed.
Unsexy businesses (line painting, private jet studios, vending machines) deliver 60-80% profit margins.
The best guests listen to the podcast first and come as genuine conversation partners, not cold interviewees.
Stable coins are the next frontier in crypto adoption; Luna moving beyond speculation to real-world usage (Korea, Mongolia).
VR adoption (Oculus 10M units, 6 games $10M+) continues despite consumer discomfort; Apple will likely dominate.
SPAC sponsors extract value regardless of outcome (20% dilution for 2% initial investment); bad risk/reward for regular investors.

banger_quotes.quote: If you can actually crack the stable coin use case, you have trillion-dollar opportunity.
You can win the game any way—rockets to Mars or white line painting.
The Winklevii end up richer than Zuck—they own 1% of all Bitcoin.
Sleep and exercise are the cure to most every problem.

numbers_dropped: Anthony von Mandel (Mark Anthony Group): $8B net worth, Mike's Hard Lemonade + White Claw = $4B revenue 2020
Vitalik Buterin: $1-2B net worth; donated $1B Shiba token to India COVID relief
Oculus: 10M units sold (~$4B hardware), outsold Xbox
Chamath SPACs: down 30% YTD vs S&P 500 up 30%
Meet Kevin YouTube portfolio: $51M, making $30M/year with 4 employees
notable: Annual awards show format with previous year's predictions reviewed; discovers many 2020 predictions came true (OnlyFans subscription model, COVID recovery), others missed (SF recovery, NFT sustainability). First appearance of Buyer.com business, which sold to Ramp 6 months after founding.
